title: Jyotish Arambha — Beginner to Navamsa
description: 7-level progressive curriculum derived from Modules 1–6 + Assignments. Each level unlocks the next when the learner passes its quiz.

levels.title: What is Jyotish?
levels.subtitle: Origins, branches, and the language of light

lessons.title: Astrology vs Astronomy
key_points: Astrology uses astronomical calculation to map charts
Vedic astrology = Jyotish = 'Light of God' (Jyoti + Isha)

lessons.title: Origins — from Sumer to India
key_points: Vedic Jyotish originates from the Vedas (Rig, Yajur, Sama, Atharva)
Jyotisha is one of the 6 Vedangas (limbs of the Veda)

levels.title: Astronomy Foundations
levels.subtitle: The sky, the zodiac belt, and celestial mechanics

lessons.title: Geocentric vs Heliocentric
key_points: Jyotish uses the Geocentric model (Earth-centred, as observed)
Ptolemy (2nd century CE) popularised the Geocentric system

lessons.title: The Zodiac Belt (Bha-Chakra)
key_points: 12 × 30° = 360°; each sign = 1 month of Sun transit
8° north-south of the ecliptic forms the luminous band

lessons.title: Tropical vs Sidereal + Ayanamsa
key_points: Sidereal (Nirayana) = star-based; Ashwini = 0° Aries — used in Jyotish
Ayanamsa = difference between the two; currently ~24°
Precession drifts the equinox ~1° every 72 years

lessons.title: The 9 Grahas — an overview
key_points: Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu
Sun and Moon = luminaries; Mars–Saturn = true planets; Rahu/Ketu = lunar nodes (mathematical)
Neptune and Uranus are NOT used in Jyotish (too slow, weak tidal force)

lessons.title: Rahu and Ketu — the lunar nodes
key_points: Mathematical points where Moon's orbit crosses the ecliptic
Rahu = North Node (ascending), Ketu = South Node (descending)
Always 180° apart, always retrograde, change signs every ~18 months, full cycle ~18.6 years
Rahu behaves like Saturn; Ketu behaves like Mars

levels.title: The 27 Nakshatras
levels.subtitle: Lunar mansions, padas, and the Vimshottari cycle

lessons.title: Why 27 Nakshatras?
key_points: Moon takes ~27 days to orbit Earth → 1 nakshatra per day
Moon defines the 27 divisions; Sun defines the 12 signs

lessons.title: Padas — the quarter divisions
key_points: Pada size = navamsa division size → D9 = nakshatra pada chart

lessons.title: The Vimshottari Lord Cycle
key_points: Order: Ketu → Venus → Sun → Moon → Mars → Rahu → Jupiter → Saturn → Mercury
Same order governs the 120-year Vimshottari Dasha

lessons.title: Pariyaya — 3 groups of 9
key_points: Each group gets same lord sequence

lessons.title: Nakshatra classifications
key_points: Nature: Chara, Sthira, Tikshna, Ugra, Laghu, Mridu, Misra
Orientation: Adho Mukha, Urdhwa Mukha, Tiryanga Mukha

lessons.title: Janma Nakshatra and Tarabalam
key_points: Janma Nakshatra = Moon's nakshatra at birth
Used for: naming, Dasha start, daily auspiciousness
Nava Tara Chakra: 9 groups from Janma — Janma, Sampat, Vipat, Kshema, Pratyak, Sadhaka, Naidhana, Mitra, Adhi-Mitra

lessons.title: Gender, Guna, Element, Varna, Direction
key_points: Sattva: Sun, Moon, Jupiter / Rajas: Mercury, Venus / Tamas: Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu

lessons.title: Sign Ownership (Rasi Swami)
key_points: Mars → Aries, Scorpio | Venus → Taurus, Libra
Mercury → Gemini, Virgo | Jupiter → Sagittarius, Pisces

lessons.title: Moolatrikona and Swakshetra
key_points: Moolatrikona = trinal strength zone within own sign
Sun: Leo 0-20°, Moon: Taurus 3-30°, Mars: Aries 0-12°
Mercury: Virgo 15-20°, Jupiter: Sag 0-10°, Venus: Libra 0-15°, Saturn: Aquarius 0-20°
Swakshetra = rest of own sign (positive sign at lower degrees)

lessons.title: Planetary Friendship (Naisargika Maitri)
key_points: Based on positions from Moolatrikona sign

lessons.title: Directional Strength (Dig Bala)
key_points: 1st house (East) = Mercury + Jupiter strong, Saturn weak
4th house (North) = Moon + Venus strong, Sun + Mars weak
7th house (West) = Saturn strong, Mercury + Jupiter weak
10th house (South) = Sun + Mars strong, Moon + Venus weak

lessons.title: Conjunctions (Yuti) and Combustion (Astanga)
key_points: Conjunction = multiple planets in same sign
Combustion orbs: Moon 12°, Mars 17°, Mercury 14°, Jupiter 11°, Venus 10°, Saturn 15°

lessons.title: Avasthas — planetary states
key_points: Baladi (5 states based on degree in sign): Bala, Kumara, Yuva, Vriddha, Mrita
Deeptadi (9 states based on dignity): Deepta, Svastha, Mudita, Shanta, Deena, Dhukita, Vikala, Kala, Kopa

levels.title: The 12 Rasis (Signs)
levels.subtitle: Qualities, elements, and the Kalapurusha mapping

lessons.title: The 12 Signs and Their Lords
key_points: Aries (Mars), Taurus (Venus), Gemini (Mercury), Cancer (Moon), Leo (Sun), Virgo (Mercury)
Libra (Venus), Scorpio (Mars), Sagittarius (Jupiter), Capricorn (Saturn), Aquarius (Saturn), Pisces (Jupiter)
Sanskrit + Tamil names — Mesha, Vrishabha, Mithuna, Karka...

lessons.title: Four Elements (Tattvas)
key_points: Ether (Akasha) — no sign; Jupiter is its rightful ruler

lessons.title: Three Modalities (Qualities)
key_points: Cardinal/Movable (Chara) — Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn — initiators
Fixed (Sthira) — Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius — stabilizers
Mutable/Dual (Dwi Swabhava) — Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces — adapters

lessons.title: Gender, Varna, Direction
key_points: Odd signs = male/krura; Even signs = female/saumya

lessons.title: Body Mapping (Kalapurusha)
key_points: Aries → Head | Taurus → Face/Neck | Gemini → Arms/Shoulders
Cancer → Chest/Heart | Leo → Stomach | Virgo → Waist/Intestines
Libra → Lower abdomen | Scorpio → Hidden parts | Sagittarius → Thighs
Capricorn → Knees | Aquarius → Calves | Pisces → Feet

lessons.title: Functional Classifications
key_points: Nature of rising: Shirshodaya (head-first, day-strong) vs Prishthodaya (back-first, night-strong)
Fruitful/Semi/Barren — Water is fruitful, Fire is barren

lessons.title: Three Lagnas: Janma, Chandra, Surya
key_points: Janma Lagna = Ascendant at birth (physical body, life direction)
Chandra Lagna = Moon's sign (mind, emotions — most used in daily Jyotish)
Surya Lagna = Sun's sign (soul, life purpose)

levels.title: The 12 Bhavas (Houses) & Chart Reading
levels.subtitle: Domains of life, classifications, and Raja Yoga

lessons.title: Signs vs Houses — the critical distinction
key_points: Signs = fixed qualities of the zodiac (universal)
Houses = life domains, rotate from Lagna (personal)
A planet's house tells WHERE it acts; its sign tells HOW it acts

lessons.title: Natural Karakas for Houses
key_points: 1st: Sun | 2nd: Jupiter | 3rd: Mars | 4th: Moon & Mercury | 5th: Jupiter
6th: Mars & Saturn | 7th: Venus | 8th: Saturn | 9th: Sun & Jupiter
10th: Sun + Jupiter + Mercury + Saturn | 11th: Jupiter | 12th: Saturn

lessons.title: Kendra, Trikona, Upachaya, Dusthana
key_points: Upachaya (3,6,10,11) — Growth houses — malefics do well

lessons.title: House Lordship (Athipathi)
key_points: A house's lord = planet ruling the sign occupying that house
Example: if Leo is 10th House, Sun becomes 10th Lord
The condition and placement of the lord determines how that domain manifests

lessons.title: Yogakaraka and Raja Yoga
key_points: Kendra + Trikona lord combinations → Raja Yoga
Yogakaraka = one planet that lords BOTH a Kendra and a Trikona
Taurus Lagna → Saturn (9+10) | Cancer → Mars (5+10) | Leo → Mars (4+9)
Libra → Saturn (4+5) | Capricorn → Venus (5+10) | Aquarius → Venus (4+9)

lessons.title: Kendradhipathi Dosa
key_points: Natural benefics lose beneficence when they rule Kendras
Applies strongly to dual-sign Lagnas: Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces
1st lord exempt if it also owns a Trikona

lessons.title: Bhavat Bhavam & Dispositors
key_points: Dispositor = lord of the sign where a planet sits

lessons.title: First Steps to Analyse a Chart
key_points: 1. House Rasi — sign occupying the house
2. House Lord — where the lord sits
3. Occupants — who is in the house
4. Aspects — who is looking at it

levels.title: Navamsa (D9) — Unlock
levels.subtitle: The 9th division: marriage, strength, and dharma
levels.unlock_requirement: Complete L1–L6 and score ≥ 70% on all quizzes
levels.source: Module 3 (Divisional Charts) + Module 5 (D9 Mapping) + Navamsa Calculator

lessons.title: What is Navamsa?
key_points: Each sign divided into 9 amsas of 3°20'

lessons.title: How to Calculate Navamsa
key_points: Fire signs → amsas start from Aries
Earth signs → amsas start from Capricorn
Air signs → amsas start from Libra
Water signs → amsas start from Cancer

lessons.title: Pada to Navamsa
key_points: Each nakshatra pada maps to one navamsa sign
Same mapping can be read from the Nakshatra Table
Ashwini Pd1 → Aries | Pd2 → Taurus | Pd3 → Gemini | Pd4 → Cancer
Ashwini Bh4 → Cancer; Bharani Pd1 → Leo, etc.

lessons.title: Vargottama and Pushkara
key_points: Vargottama = same sign in D1 and D9 (extra strength)

lessons.title: Uses of D9
key_points: Karakamsha Lagna = sign of Atmakaraka in D9; 12th from it reveals Ishta Devata (Jaimini)

lessons.title: Beginner Interpretation Workflow
key_points: 1. Locate each planet's nakshatra and pada
2. Map the pada to its navamsa sign
3. Check if any planet is Vargottama
4. Check if debilitated D1 planets become exalted in D9
5. Read the 7th house of D9 for spouse indications

crash_course_7_day.title: 7-Day Crash Course
crash_course_7_day.description: Fast-track path through the levels for motivated learners
